items.headline: City releases update on children and youth services master plan
items.summary: San José City Council reviewed progress on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan, including reports on youth empowerment programs, the Safe Summer Initiative Grant, and youth intervention services. This plan shapes how the city supports young residents through various city programs and partnerships.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street was cancelled on April 8, 2026, with no new hearing date yet scheduled. Residents waiting to learn about this development will need to watch for future meeting notices.

items.headline: City Council Approves Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents should monitor future updates to learn what infrastructure or improvement project this contract covers.

items.headline: Mexican Heritage Plaza receives state arts and culture funding
items.summary: The City Council approved a California Natural Resources Agency grant for the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza, supporting expanded cultural programming in San José.

items.headline: Active Transportation Plan Phase 2 update with revised scoring criteria
items.summary: The city presented updates to its Active Transportation Plan including Phase 2 findings and new scoring criteria for evaluating projects. This plan will shape where Cupertino invests in biking, walking, and transit infrastructure over coming years.

items.headline: Council votes to change vacancy procedures for filling open seats
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are filled, reducing what appears to be a waiting period or procedural requirement. This could affect how quickly the city can appoint new council members if seats become vacant.

items.headline: New 65-unit senior affordable housing complex coming to East St John
items.summary: Trillium is building a $8.8M senior apartment building with 65 units at 678 E St John St, addressing the city's shortage of affordable housing for older residents. The project is 100% deed-restricted affordable housing.

items.headline: Trimble Summerhill residential development adds 6 new homes to Seely Avenue
items.summary: Two permits issued for a 6-plex new construction project at Seely Av valued at $2M, representing a modest but solid residential infill project in an established neighborhood.
